steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word 'but' signals a contrast: the habitat explanation for the species' absence is being challenged by the fact that the region is 'notoriously inaccessible.' The correct answer must explain how inaccessibility undermines the habitat claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'researchers may have confused this species with a similar one': species confusion is unrelated to the region's inaccessibility. ✗
  • Choice B — 'limited access to the region has yielded more observed species, not fewer': inverts the logic — inaccessibility would produce fewer observations, not more. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the region may not have been thoroughly explored, making the absence a product of incomplete searching': directly connects inaccessibility to the possibility that the species simply hasn't been found yet, contrasting the habitat explanation. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the habitat is indeed unsuitable, confirming the original explanation': restates the very claim the 'but' is meant to question. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
